source-discipline.reason: Every example gap cited in the Context section (GAP-069, GAP-074, GAP-076, GAP-267, GAP-275, GAP-012, GAP-027, GAP-040, GAP-284, GAP-283) was looked up directly in the current OPEN gap set during Pass 1 read of the gaps table. Cited owner directives ('owner pivot turn 14, 2026-05-23') trace to session_2026-05-23-bpc-rewrite-phase-b-closure.md remaining-open-work section. No invented gap IDs, no invented sessions, no invented directives. Doctrinal commitments 2/3/5 cited by number rather than fabricated paraphrase.

canonical-workplan.reason: DR refines what 'B.11 citation mining' means for the gap-driven track without altering workplan structure. PI rule #6 holds the workplan as canonical; this DR cites workplan §B.11 as the host phase, owner's 2026-05-23 turn-14 pivot as the policy trigger, and does not propose phase reordering or new phase introduction.

evidence-verification-gate.reason: DR §Decision/Output writes #2 enforces rule #10: candidates returned by mining are recorded as DOIs in gap_mining.candidate_dois and are NOT INSERTed to evidence_sources without verification. Mirrors the supersession_check.superseding_dois pattern (DR-2026-05-24). Rule #10's existence-and-content gate is preserved; this DR adds an evidence-discovery dimension without weakening verification.

best-practice-supersession.reason: Design pattern explicitly inherited from DR-2026-05-24: append-only per-attempt rows, MAX(attempt_at) as operative outcome, semiannual sweep cadence, DOI-only candidates not auto-promoted. DR §Interlocks bullet 4 names the join: discoveries that supersede existing anchors create joint gap_mining + supersession_check rows describing orthogonal dimensions.

adherence-logging-and-attestation.reason: This attestation IS the rule firing. The DR's commit carries [DOCTRINE: 61c7f95] before the timestamp per v10.14 canonical form. The skill file deliverable in the next session will additionally require attestation; the DR commits to that in §Implementation deliverables.

gap-driven-mining.reason: This DR introduces the rule. It is in-scope for itself by definition — the DR is the authorizing instrument. Per DR-2026-05-24's same self-reference pattern.

deviations.reason: DR is silent on BATCH-mode citation-miner interaction: if a slug-level citation-miner pass discovers a source that closes a previously-mining-addressable gap, the gap-closure handshake is not specified. Mitigation: the skill file deliverable in Pass 2b (next session) addresses this as part of the BATCH/INLINE mode mechanics; the DR ships with this gap acknowledged. Evidence: decisions/DR-2026-05-26-gap-driven-mining-protocol.md §Triggers and §Implementation-deliverables next-session item 4.

The Q14 adversarial pass on the 2026-07-13 ratification execution (workplan/ratification-execution-register-2026-07-13.md, B-scope finding 1) found this DR was ratified ACCEPTED on 2026-07-13 while its own Status section's adoption gate remains unmet: gap_mining has 0 rows and all 297 gaps rows have mining_addressability IS NULL (verified by direct query against data/guidebook.db). Migration 017 and the skill file are in place; only the worked-example pilot and the three non-error-outcome demonstrations never ran. The owner's 'ratify all' directive is recorded as authorizing this override explicitly, not as an oversight -- but the gap itself was previously untracked in the execution register and is now queue item Q21. Evidence: decisions/DR-2026-05-26-gap-driven-mining-protocol.md#status (footer, corrected same commit as this re-attestation).

bias_direction: I authored this DR after the owner delegated workstream choice with the criterion 'long-term integrity.' My natural pull is toward making the protocol look like it interlocks cleanly with every existing rule (#7, #8, #10, DR-2026-05-24) — the interlock framing is rhetorically protective: it makes the protocol harder to challenge because challenges have to navigate the rule mesh. The bias direction is over-claiming interlock cleanliness; the empirical test (whether the interlocks actually fire on real gaps in pilot) is deferred to Pass 2b's worked example. A more parsimonious DR would have left the interlock claims softer ('relates to' rather than 'enforces') until empirical validation. [RE-ATTESTATION 2026-06-11 per PI rule #11a: originally attested by session_2026-05-26-gap-driven-mining-protocol-design at doctrine 61c7f95; re-checked and re-attested at 3da73bd by session_2026-06-11-stage-4.3-gate-closure after doctrine delta 3fb2882 (Stage-1.1 tier-hierarchy revision: SR/meta T3->T2, Tier 1 expanded to admit directly-relevant high-control non-OT primary research, Tier 3 redefined as lower-control primary, Tiers 4-6 walled off). Assessment: Protocol substance (anchor-keyed, tier-prioritized citation mining) complies. The 'Tier 1-3' anchor-source range reflects pre-Stage-1.1 composition (SR/meta now Tier 2). Stale reference, not a compliance defect.] [RE-ATTESTATION 2026-07-13 per Mode 3 item 3 (skills/integrity-protocol_SKILL.md): the Q14 adversarial pass found this DR's ratification outran its own execution -- see the new deviations[] entry above. This is exactly the failure mode this attestation's own independent_reviewer_counterclaim point (d) named as a risk ("deferring the triage pass means this DR ships without the queue-size data needed to test whether the protocol is operationally tractable") -- the risk materialized: ratification happened before the pilot the DR's own adoption gate requires. Tracked as queue item Q21, not silently absorbed.]
independent_reviewer_counterclaim: An outside reviewer would observe: (a) the 'default class derived from gaps.skill' claim is theoretical — no empirical test that, e.g., functional-deficit-auditor gaps are 100% ADDRESSABLE, so the default class may produce wrong defaults at scale; (b) the search matrix is borrowed from supersession-audit's structure without empirical validation that the same tool prioritization works for the different evidence-discovery problem gap-driven mining poses (anchor-less search is shape-different from anchor-traversal search); (c) the 6-month re-eligibility cadence is parameter-cloned from DR-2026-05-24 with no evidence-based reason it's the right cadence for null gap-mining attempts; (d) deferring the triage pass means this DR ships without the queue-size data needed to test whether the protocol is operationally tractable. Counter: (a) the triage data migration logs per-gap rationale and surfaces default-mismatches empirically; (b) Pass 2b's worked-example pilot tests the matrix on a real gap and revises if it returns nulls; (c) 6 months is the project's existing maintenance rhythm and using a different cadence here would introduce inconsistency without offsetting benefit; (d) the queue-size question is part of pilot validation and the DR explicitly defers operational scaling to that pilot per §Status's adoption criteria.
